domain: Andamanese Onge/Jarawa oral tradition (Andaman Islands, India; ~96 Onge + ~250 Jarawa surviving populations; no written tradition): purely oral knowledge transmission via storytelling + songs + dances chained to named ancestors; redundant transmission both vertical (parent→child) and horizontal (peer); 2004 tsunami survival demonstrates environmental-trigger retrieval: Onge stories of 'huge shaking + high wall of water' preserved across many generations and fired correctly when ground-shake sensory cue occurred.
specific_mechanism: ANDAMANESE-ORAL-ANCESTOR-CHAIN-VERTICAL-HORIZONTAL-DUPLICATION-ENVIRONMENTAL-TRIGGER-RETRIEVAL: LLM memory-architecture mechanism with (1) ancestor-anchored memory chain M_anc: each memory entry tagged with named ancestor (provenance handle); entries form per-ancestor linked chain; (2) vertical-horizontal dual-duplication D_2: 2 independent storage paths — vertical (deeper-layer parent-to-child propagation across pretraining) + horizontal (per-layer peer-to-peer broadcast across attention-heads); redundant against single-path loss; (3) environmental-trigger retrieval gate g_env: external sensory-cue token (e.g., 'shake'/'rumble') activates ancestor-tagged retrieval, bypassing semantic match alone; (4) survival-criticality priority weighting w_surv: each memory entry has criticality score; high-criticality entries get N-copies redundancy boost across vertical + horizontal stores; (5) generational depth decay δ_gen: per-generation half-life on access count; named-ancestor handle boosts activation. Differs from R529 CUNEIFORM-2-AXIS-WEDGE-DEPTH-ORIENTATION-MEMORY (2-axis K-depth × M-orientation + per-tablet page + reed K-cohort + baking-confirm) and R542 XIUHMOLPILLI-DUAL-CYCLE-365-260-ENGAGED-GEARS-MEMORY (dual-cycle 365+260 + engaged-gears 52-year + fire-renewal + nēmontēmi GC + 20×13 cross-indexed) by ancestor-anchored chain + vertical-horizontal duplication + environmental-trigger retrieval + survival-criticality priority + generational depth decay.
llm_application: ANDAMANESE-ORAL-ANCESTOR-CHAIN-VERTICAL-HORIZONTAL-DUPLICATION-ENVIRONMENTAL-TRIGGER-RETRIEVAL LLM mechanism + ancestor-anchored memory chain M_anc named-provenance per-ancestor linked + vertical-horizontal dual-duplication D_2 deeper-layer pretraining + per-layer attention-head broadcast + environmental-trigger retrieval gate g_env sensory-cue ancestor-tagged activation + survival-criticality priority weighting w_surv N-copies + generational depth decay δ_gen per-generation half-life. Differs from R529 CUNEIFORM (closest — 2-axis K-depth × M-orientation + baking-confirm). R542 XIUHMOLPILLI (closest — dual-cycle 365+260 + 52-year engaged-gears). Differs from Memory Survey 2603.07670 + Episodic Memory 2502.06975 + Long-Term Memory 2510.27246 + MemAgents ICLR 2026 by ancestor-anchored chain + vertical-horizontal dual-duplication + environmental-trigger retrieval + survival-criticality priority + generational depth decay. Differs by ANCESTOR-ANCHORED MEMORY CHAIN + VERTICAL-HORIZONTAL DUAL-DUPLICATION + ENVIRONMENTAL-TRIGGER RETRIEVAL + SURVIVAL-CRITICALITY PRIORITY + GENERATIONAL DEPTH DECAY.
novelty_claim: Memory Survey 2603.07670 + Episodic Memory 2502.06975 + Long-Term Memory 2510.27246 + MemAgents ICLR 2026 cover episodic + hierarchical memory + survival paradigm. ANDAMANESE-ORAL-ANCESTOR-CHAIN-VERTICAL-HORIZONTAL-DUPLICATION-ENVIRONMENTAL-TRIGGER-RETRIEVAL adds (a) ancestor-anchored chain + (b) vertical-horizontal dual-duplication + (c) environmental-trigger retrieval + (d) survival-criticality priority + (e) generational depth decay. Recombination — borderline lookup.
